title: GLLVM mission control
subtitle: Local finish board for the gllvmTMB + GLLVM.jl twin. It separates branch health, draft PR checks, bridge admission, simulation evidence, public documentation, and release readiness.
source: local worktrees, handover checkpoint, GitHub CLI, and local LaunchAgent log

claim_guard.title: Claim boundary
claim_guard.text: PR green != bridge complete != release ready != scientific coverage passed. The Julia bridge is draft and partial unless a matrix row names covered evidence.

truth_cards.label: Local workspace
truth_cards.note: Dashboard evidence refresh is tracked on the #489 branch; recovery checkpoints remain untracked local handoff notes.

truth_cards.label: Draft PR
truth_cards.note: Draft PR #489 is clean and both current checks are green at 03fdda1; it remains partial and not release-ready.

truth_cards.label: Main full-check
truth_cards.note: Main R-CMD-check 27720099917 and pkgdown 27720668325 are green at 0567cd7. Newer scheduled full-check 27752749643 also completed successfully on windows-latest, ubuntu-latest, and macos-latest at the same head. This confirms main CI health only; it does not close the bridge, release, or scientific-coverage gates.

truth_cards.label: Power pilot
truth_cards.note: Run 27722546237 succeeded and pushed 5969f6f; run 27735802023 cancelled without branch movement; Run 27752884846 completed success at 2026-06-18T14:07:44Z with 51/51 jobs successful and pushed power-pilot-results 1a2aac6 (run 110). The remote store now has 48 cells, 29176 / 480000 reps, 1/48 cells at cap, signal mean coverage 0.748, pass94 3/24, pass95 2/24, null mean coverage-under-null 0.426, 28 flagged cells, and 28 coverage anomalies. Fisher/Curie scoring still blocks coverage or power promotion. Local iter 24 remains process evidence only.

truth_cards.label: Psi cleanup
truth_cards.note: First implementation slice reframed api-keyword-grid, covariance-correlation, model-selection-latent-rank, and cross-lineage-coevolution around explicit Psi versus standalone indep(). pkgdown::build_articles(lazy = FALSE) rendered the touched pages, pkgdown::check_pkgdown() passed, and stale scans only hit intentional guard/ledger text. Broader article estate remains one-decision-at-a-time.

truth_cards.label: Laplace / VA
truth_cards.note: gllvmTMB production estimation uses the TMB Laplace path for latent/random effects, with coverage still family-row specific. Current GLLVM.jl has exact Gaussian, Laplace one-part non-Gaussian families, and dedicated two-part Laplace fitters. VA is not production in either current checkout: gllvmTMB Design 72 parks the VA prototype, and current GLLVM.jl exposes no VA/ELBO API. This is a planned/future lane only if reopened with validation rows.

truth_cards.label: Paper 2 kernels
truth_cards.note: Fixed named multi-kernel engine slice landed locally as latent-only: KER-03 covered, COE-03 remains partial, and COE-04 is now partial for near-orthogonal Gaussian latent-only component recovery, two-cell moderate-overlap recovery, high-overlap exact-duplicate and diagonal-shrink near-duplicate collapse-equivalence, fitted-object kernel-similarity diagnostics, two-sided selective absence, block-null smoke plus a small null/signal grid, fixed-rho sensitivity, fixed-rho Gamma_effect extraction, and high-overlap fit/extract_Gamma warning language. The fixed-rho grid detects positive cross signal versus rho = 0 but does not estimate rho. Broader/harder moderate-overlap calibration, broader high-overlap truth-recovery/failure calibration beyond the collapse-equivalence and warning gates, broader null-threshold calibration, formal rho profile/estimation support, interval, and mixed/non-Gaussian gates remain blocked. kernel_unique()/*_unique() stays compatibility syntax for now and should be redesigned/deprecated after this arc; the Paper 2 multi-kernel path remains latent-only. This is engine/extractor evidence only; it is not bridge completion, release readiness, or scientific coverage.

truth_cards.label: Next decision
truth_cards.note: GLLVM.jl #95 has landed and main CI is green; #101 merge-ref and local live JuliaCall bridge suites passed. Maintainer-approved close/reopen triggered fresh #101 PR CI run 27763712855 and Documenter run 27763712914 at f7be594; both completed successfully and #101 is clean, but #489 still needs an explicit landing/split decision before bridge claims widen.

active_work.text: #489 is clean and green at 03fdda1 and #101 fresh PR CI is now green at f7be594, but keep the bridge draft/partial until the landing/split decision and claim gates close.

gllvmTMB #489 R-CMD/recovery, GLLVM.jl main CI/Documenter, #101 fresh PR CI, local bridge suites, and main scheduled full-check 27752749643 are green. Fresh #101 PR CI run 27763712855 and Documenter run 27763712914 both completed success at f7be594. Full-check 27752749643 completed successfully on windows-latest, ubuntu-latest, and macos-latest at 0567cd7. Run 27752884846 completed success at 2026-06-18T14:07:44Z with 51/51 jobs successful and pushed power-pilot-results 1a2aac6 (run 110). The remote store now has 48 cells, 29176 / 480000 reps, 1/48 cells at cap, signal mean coverage 0.748, pass94 3/24, pass95 2/24, null mean coverage-under-null 0.426, 28 flagged cells, and 28 coverage anomalies. Fisher/Curie scoring still blocks coverage or power promotion. Local iter 24 reached 383510 / 480000 reps as process evidence.
Keeping all bridge, interval, release, and power wording bounded by row-level evidence.

Article council slices 1-5A are reflected in source, and the first Psi cleanup anchor sweep now renders: standalone diagonal teaching moves to indep(), while unique() is framed as explicit Psi in decomposed models.

kernel_*() remains the general API; relmat remains compatibility sugar. Fixed named multi-kernel tiers now have a local TMB engine slice, two-cell moderate-overlap recovery, fixed-rho sensitivity, fixed-rho Gamma_effect extraction, and a small COE-04 null/signal grid, while unique()/*_unique() stays compatibility syntax for this arc; the post-arc cleanup should deprecate or supersede *_unique() teaching, with standalone diagonal teaching already pointing to indep().

Laplace/VA capability audit added: gllvmTMB has the production TMB Laplace route but row-specific validation gates remain; GLLVM.jl has exact Gaussian, Laplace one-part families, and dedicated two-part Laplace fitters; VA is parked/prototype-only for gllvmTMB and absent as a production GLLVM.jl API. Planned gaps now include R VA revival, Julia VA/ELBO design, two-part bridge parity, and Julia planned families such as Delta-Gamma, ZIP/ZINB, Tweedie, and Exponential/public dispatch.

matrix.evidence: PR #489 is clean and green at 03fdda1: coevolution recovery 27727834498 and R-CMD-check 27727834488 both succeeded. GLLVM.jl #101 merge-ref 2cd8563 focused bridge slice passed 641/641 locally with Julia 1.10. gllvmTMB no-Julia bridge suite passed; live JuliaCall bridge suite passed locally against GLLVM.jl f7be594 with juliaup on PATH. Maintainer-approved close/reopen triggered fresh #101 PR CI run 27763712855 and Documenter run 27763712914 at the same f7be594 head; both completed success and #101 is clean.
matrix.next_gate: Decide the #489 landing/split path and keep claims partial until bridge, release, and scientific coverage gates close.

matrix.evidence: Point rows allowed; correlation intervals and richer extractors remain gated.
matrix.next_gate: Local live JuliaCall suite passed; interval wording remains blocked until #101 PR CI and interval semantics are covered.

matrix.evidence: Current gllvmTMB uses the TMB random-effects/Laplace path as the production estimator for latent and random-effect models. This covers the implemented engine path, but it does not mean every family/distribution has equal simulation, interval, bridge, or public-article coverage. Family depth remains validation-row specific, and delta/hurdle/mixed-family latent-scale interpretation stays guarded.
matrix.next_gate: Keep Laplace claims tied to docs/design/35-validation-debt-register.md rows and per-family tests; do not advertise all-distribution scientific coverage from the shared estimator alone.

matrix.evidence: Design 72 records VA as a parked feasibility/prototype lane: the Phase 1 separate-DLL experiment converged where Laplace failed and matched Laplace when identifiable, but it did not solve under-identification and no method = "VA" production API is merged.
matrix.next_gate: Only revive VA as a separate method-development lane with explicit ELBO design, family coverage, benchmarks, warning semantics, and validation rows.

matrix.capability: GLLVM.jl exact Gaussian and Laplace family surface
matrix.evidence: Current GLLVM.jl documents exact closed-form Gaussian fits, Laplace one-part non-Gaussian fits for Binomial, Poisson, NegativeBinomial, Beta, Ordinal, and Gamma, plus dedicated two-part Laplace fitters for Delta-lognormal, Hurdle-Poisson, and Hurdle-NB. The gllvmTMB bridge capability table remains partial: fixed-effect X, masks, CI payloads, residuals, simulation, and mixed-family support differ by family, with mixed-family currently a balanced no-X/no-mask/no-CI route.
matrix.next_gate: Close planned Julia family and bridge gaps one row at a time: Delta-Gamma, ZIP/ZINB, Tweedie, Exponential/public row, unified two-part dispatch, structured non-Gaussian dependence, formula parity, CI/post-fit parity, and mixed-family masks/X/CI.

matrix.evidence: The current GLLVM.jl checkout includes no production VA/ELBO estimator or public VA method switch. Older handoff notes about VA branches are not current-source evidence.
matrix.next_gate: If VA is revived, make it a separate Julia design and validation lane before any bridge or public capability claim.

matrix.evidence: LaunchAgent latest logged summary is iter 24 at 383510 / 480000 reps, 0/48 cells at cap, 0 errored cells, signal mean coverage 0.753, pass94 3/24, pass95 2/24, and null mean coverage-under-null 0.425. Status helper at 05:17 MDT confirmed the same totals, STOP flag absent, parent loop plus all ten workers alive, and nine workers active. Local scoring audit still shows low coverage and invalid zero-reject interpretation for Sigma_unit_diag.

Run 27722546237 completed successfully and pushed 5969f6f (run 108) to power-pilot-results; scoring blocked promotion. Scheduled run 27735802023 completed cancelled at 0567cd7 with no branch movement. Completed power-pilot run 27752884846 succeeded at main 0567cd7 and moved power-pilot-results 5969f6f -> 1a2aac6 (1a2aac654e877a34b3e590f0269d10cca05a43e7). The scored remote run-110 store has 48 cells, 29176 / 480000 reps, 1/48 cells at cap, signal mean coverage 0.748, pass94 3/24, pass95 2/24, null mean coverage-under-null 0.426, 28 flagged cells, and 28 coverage anomalies. The scoring audit, run against the remote run-110 store plus the local store, blocks promotion: gaussian-d1-n150-sig0p0 coverage 0.390 with one-sided misses, nbinom2-d2-n50-sig0p0 coverage 0.899 with 76% non-PD, and binomial_probit-d1-n50-sig0p2 coverage 0.959 but 34% non-PD and extremely wide intervals. CI-excludes-zero remains diagnostic only for Sigma_unit_diag, not valid Type-I error.

matrix.evidence: Gaussian lane covered; non-Gaussian guard remains reserved.
matrix.next_gate: Family-specific recovery evidence before guard relaxation.

matrix.capability: Known-V, proportional, and scalar hard-inference lanes

matrix.evidence: Subrows stronger than parent rows; register reconciliation needed.

Local engine slice adds two or more named dense kernel_latent() tiers over the same grouping levels, with per-tier K_r, Lambda_r, and latent field. The Paper 2 path is latent-only; explicit kernel_unique()/Psi is deferred because it is a poor default for non-Gaussian and cross-family coevolution. Evidence: non-heavy devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") passed 112, heavy GLLVMTMB_HEAVY_TESTS=1 devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") passed 138, and pkgdown::check_pkgdown() passed with Pandoc on PATH. The one-name KER-02 equivalence path remains green.
matrix.next_gate: Sparse/generic kernel unification, crossed/different groups, kernel_dep multi-tier, and source-specific phylo/animal/spatial re-pointing remain future rows.

matrix.evidence: Fixed two-component named-kernel fits now work locally and extract separate component shared Sigma/Gamma_shape blocks by name. For tiers built from make_cross_kernel(), extract_Gamma(scale = "effect") returns fixed-rho Gamma_effect = rho_r * Gamma_shape_r. C3.1 is latent-only for Paper 2; C3.2 still guards legacy two kernel_unique tiers without replication. COE-04 near-orthogonal heavy recovery, two-cell moderate-overlap recovery, high-overlap exact-duplicate and diagonal-shrink near-duplicate collapse-equivalence, two-sided selective-absence, block-null smoke plus a small null/signal grid, fixed-rho sensitivity, fixed-rho effect-scale extraction, and high-overlap fit/extract_Gamma warning fixtures now pass, but this remains partial and does not prove explicit Psi support, broad/harder moderate-overlap calibration, broad high-overlap truth-recovery/failure calibration, broader null-threshold calibration, formal rho profile/estimation support, or intervals.
matrix.next_gate: Broader COE-04 recovery/separation grid before scientific coverage or public Paper 2 promotion.

matrix.evidence: Multi-kernel fits now carry fit$kernel_diagnostics with pairwise off-diagonal Frobenius-style kernel similarity and overlap classes. Fast diagnostic evidence classifies low-overlap versus high-overlap K_phy/K_non pairs; high-overlap fitted tier pairs warn that component-specific Gamma_shape separation is weak evidence while still fitting; and extract_Gamma(level = ...) repeats that warning when the requested component participates in a high-overlap pair while still returning the point block. Heavy near-orthogonal Gaussian latent-only evidence passes: full two-component fit beats either one-component fit by >50 log-likelihood units, component-specific extract_Gamma() recovers each Gamma_shape truth with correlation >0.95 while not matching the other component, and extract_Gamma(scale = "effect") returns fixed-rho Gamma_effect = rho_r * Gamma_shape_r for each recorded cross-kernel tier. A two-cell moderate-overlap grid blends the non association pattern 30% and 35% toward the phy pattern, lands both cells in the moderate overlap class, and recovers both component Gamma_shape matrices with cross-component matches below 0.25; an exploratory 40% blend degraded and stays outside the claim. High-overlap collapse-equivalence gates cover an exact duplicate pair and a diagonal-shrink near-duplicate pair, verify the separated two-tier fit is not materially better than one collapsed rank-2 kernel tier, and keep separated Gamma extraction warned while collapsed extraction is quiet and finite. Two-sided selective-absence gates collapse whichever component has true zero loadings below 1e-3 while recovering the present component. A block-null smoke gate sets both loading blocks to zero, collapses both extracted Gamma_shape norms below 1e-3, and keeps the full model within 3 log-likelihood units of an intercept-only fit; the small null/signal grid repeats null collapse across three seeds and contrasts it with two medium-signal fixtures that recover both component Gamma_shape blocks. A fixed-rho sensitivity grid refits the phy component over rho = c(0, 0.25, 0.55, 0.85) while holding the non component fixed: the planted rho = 0.55 and the best positive-rho fit strongly beat rho = 0, but the best grid point may sit at the high edge, so this is sensitivity/cross-signal evidence only, not rho estimation. Focused tests pass 47 non-heavy / 171 heavy; aggregate kernel|coevolution tests pass 142 non-heavy / 291 heavy. Broader/harder moderate-overlap calibration, broader high-overlap truth-recovery/failure calibration beyond the collapse-equivalence and warning gates, broader null-threshold calibration, formal rho profile/estimation support, and interval calibration remain blocked.
matrix.next_gate: Extend the Paper 2 recovery grid to broader/harder moderate-overlap calibration, calibrated high-overlap failure/recovery behavior, broader null-threshold calibration, formal rho profile/estimation support, and interval calibration before any full scientific-coverage language moves.

matrix.evidence: First implementation slice edited api-keyword-grid.Rmd, covariance-correlation.Rmd, model-selection-latent-rank.Rmd, and cross-lineage-coevolution.Rmd. The rendered pages now teach indep() for standalone marginal-only diagonal models, reserve unique() for explicit Psi in latent()+unique() decompositions, and treat kernel_unique() in the coevolution fixture as optional Psi rather than the source of Gamma or proof of Paper 2 Option B. pkgdown::build_articles(lazy = FALSE) and pkgdown::check_pkgdown() passed.
matrix.next_gate: Continue the article sweep through response-families, binary/JSDM, phylogenetic, animal, and advanced-method pages one decision at a time.

matrix.capability: Missing-data closure beyond covered local rows
matrix.evidence: MIS-23..MIS-31 covered locally; MIS-32 remains blocked/deferred.
matrix.next_gate: Rose/Fisher closure audit, not chat-based issue closure.

Use default GLLVM.jl fitting-path wording only; do not imply selectable Julia-side algorithms.
Run-110 remote store still has only 1/48 cells at cap; persistence success is process evidence, not coverage proof.
Run 108 persisted fresh results at 5969f6f, but scoring showed pass95 0/24, 28 flagged cells, and 27 coverage anomalies; no coverage/power promotion.

Scheduled run 27735802023 completed cancelled at 0567cd7: shard jobs succeeded, persist and summary cancelled after the 20-minute job limit, and power-pilot-results stayed at 5969f6f; no new persisted store or scoring evidence.
Run 27752884846 completed success and pushed 1a2aac6, but scoring audit shows undercoverage/high non-PD/diagnostic-only zero-reject issues; no coverage or power promotion.
No issue is closed from dashboard text or chat memory.

unique()/*_unique() stays compatibility syntax during the current arc; after the coevolution/Psi cleanup, *_unique() should move to a deprecation or supersession plan. Public teaching now prefers indep() for standalone diagonal models and reserves explicit Psi only where evidence supports it.

Laplace is production estimator evidence, not all-family scientific coverage. VA is not production in either current checkout; treat it as a planned lane only if reopened with explicit validation rows.

evidence.text: Expanded the COE-04 high-overlap collapse-equivalence gate from an exact duplicate kernel pair to exact duplicate plus diagonal-shrink near-duplicate cases. Both cases warn on the separated two-tier fit, converge, stay within 2 log-likelihood units of one collapsed rank-2 kernel tier, warn for component-specific extract_Gamma(level = ...), and keep the collapsed Gamma extraction quiet and finite. This is high-overlap failure/collapse discipline, not high-overlap truth recovery. Checks: coevolution-two-kernel PASS 47 non-heavy / PASS 171 heavy; kernel|coevolution PASS 142 non-heavy / PASS 291 heavy. PR green != bridge complete != release ready != scientific coverage passed.

Expanded COE-04 moderate-overlap evidence from one conservative moderate-edge fixture to a two-cell grid: non_association_blend = 0.30 at seed 2401 and 0.35 at seed 2402 both land in the moderate overlap class, converge, beat one-component fits by >50 log-likelihood units, recover both component Gamma_shape matrices above 0.95 correlation, and keep cross-component matches below 0.25. An exploratory 0.40 blend degraded (phy Gamma_shape correlation about 0.88 and cross-match above 0.25), so harder moderate overlap remains outside the claim. Checks: devtools::test(filter = "coevolution-two-kernel") -> PASS 47 with 8 expected heavy skips; GLLVMTMB_HEAVY_TESTS=1 devtools::test(filter = "coevolution-two-kernel") -> PASS 158; devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") -> PASS 142 with 11 expected heavy skips; GLLVMTMB_HEAVY_TESTS=1 devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") -> PASS 278. PR green != bridge complete != release ready != scientific coverage passed.

Added a heavy fixed-rho sensitivity grid to COE-04. The fixture rebuilds the phy cross-kernel at rho = c(0, 0.25, 0.55, 0.85) while holding the non component fixed, verifies all fits converge, and checks that the planted rho = 0.55 and the best positive-rho fit strongly beat the block-null rho = 0 fit. The test deliberately does not require the best grid point to recover the planted rho because fixed kernel strength and loading magnitudes can trade off; this is sensitivity/cross-signal evidence, not rho estimation, profile intervals, bridge completion, release readiness, or scientific coverage. Checks: devtools::test(filter = "coevolution-two-kernel") -> PASS 47 with 8 expected heavy skips; GLLVMTMB_HEAVY_TESTS=1 devtools::test(filter = "coevolution-two-kernel") -> PASS 145; devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") -> PASS 142 with 11 expected heavy skips; GLLVMTMB_HEAVY_TESTS=1 devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") -> PASS 265. PR green != bridge complete != release ready != scientific coverage passed.

Added estimation-method rows to the mission-control matrix. gllvmTMB has the production TMB Laplace estimator path for latent/random-effect models, but family, interval, bridge, and public-article claims remain validation-row specific. gllvmTMB VA remains parked in Design 72: the Phase 1 prototype was a separate DLL/branch experiment and no method = "VA" production API is merged. Current GLLVM.jl has exact Gaussian, Laplace one-part non-Gaussian families (Binomial, Poisson, NegativeBinomial, Beta, Ordinal, Gamma), and dedicated two-part Laplace fitters (Delta-lognormal, Hurdle-Poisson, Hurdle-NB). Current GLLVM.jl does not expose production VA/ELBO. Planned/gated gaps include Delta-Gamma, ZIP/ZINB, Tweedie, Exponential/public row, unified two-part dispatch, structured non-Gaussian dependence, formula parity, CI/post-fit parity, mixed-family masks/X/CI, and any future VA lane. PR green != bridge complete != release ready != scientific coverage passed.

Added fixed-rho Gamma_effect extraction without widening the scientific claim: make_cross_kernel() now records supplied rho metadata on K_star, fitted single- and multi-kernel tiers preserve rho in fit$kernel_levels, and extract_Gamma(scale = "effect") returns rho * Gamma_shape only for tiers built from make_cross_kernel(); generic kernels fail loudly and should use scale = "shape". The coevolution teaching fixture was regenerated so ex$K_star carries the same metadata. Checks: devtools::document(quiet = TRUE) regenerated extract_Gamma.Rd and make_cross_kernel.Rd; devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") -> PASS 142 with 10 expected heavy skips; GLLVMTMB_HEAVY_TESTS=1 devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") -> PASS 257. This is fixed-kernel shape/effect plumbing only, not rho estimation, interval coverage, non-Gaussian/cross-family coverage, bridge completion, release readiness, or scientific coverage. kernel_unique()/*_unique() remains compatibility syntax now and post-arc deprecation/redesign work remains open. PR green != bridge complete != release ready != scientific coverage passed.

Added the COE-04 small null/signal grid on top of the high-overlap collapse-equivalence gate: three near-orthogonal block-null seeds keep both component Gamma_shape norms below 1e-3 and full-versus-intercept likelihood gain below 3, while two medium-signal fixtures recover both component Gamma_shape blocks above 0.90 correlation and beat either one-component fit by more than 100 log-likelihood units. Checks: devtools::test(filter = "coevolution-two-kernel") -> PASS 47 with 7 expected heavy skips; GLLVMTMB_HEAVY_TESTS=1 devtools::test(filter = "coevolution-two-kernel") -> PASS 134; devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") -> PASS 133 with 10 expected heavy skips; GLLVMTMB_HEAVY_TESTS=1 devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") -> PASS 243. This is small-grid claim-boundary evidence, not broad null calibration, high-overlap truth recovery, or interval/rho evidence. PR green != bridge complete != release ready != scientific coverage passed.

Added extractor-level high-overlap guard: extract_Gamma(level = ...) now warns when the requested fixed kernel component participates in a high-overlap pair, while still returning the point Gamma_shape block for inspection. Moderate two-kernel extraction remains quiet. Checks: devtools::test(filter = "coevolution-two-kernel") -> PASS 47 with 5 expected heavy skips; GLLVMTMB_HEAVY_TESTS=1 devtools::test(filter = "coevolution-two-kernel") -> PASS 99; devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") -> PASS 133 with 8 expected heavy skips; GLLVMTMB_HEAVY_TESTS=1 devtools::test(filter = "kernel|coevolution") -> PASS 208. This closes warning/claim-boundary evidence only; high-overlap recovery/failure calibration remains open. PR green != bridge complete != release ready != scientific coverage passed.

First anchor sweep implemented: api-keyword-grid now recommends indep() for standalone diagonal models and unique() for explicit Psi; covariance-correlation is reframed around explicit Psi; model-selection-latent-rank uses indep() for the d = 0 diagonal baseline; cross-lineage-coevolution centers kernel_latent()/extract_Gamma() and describes kernel_unique() as fixture Psi. pkgdown::build_articles(lazy = FALSE), pkgdown::check_pkgdown(), HTML rg checks, JSON validation, and git diff --check passed. This is article-claim cleanup only, not bridge, release, or scientific-coverage completion.

Current checkout confirms the one-kernel Design 65 coevolution workflow is implemented and still green: C0 make_cross_kernel/prototype, C1 single named dense kernel equivalence, C2 point-estimate extract_Gamma known-Gamma recovery, C2 article fixture, and C3 guardrail tests passed with GLLVMTMB_HEAVY_TESTS=1 (`FAIL 0 | WARN 0 | SKIP 0 | PASS 125`). The full Paper 2 Option B model is not finished: two independent named kernels, estimated/profiled rho, Gamma_shape/Gamma_effect, bootstrap intervals, mixed-family recovery, and two-kernel identifiability simulations remain future gates.
